title: Canine lateral thoracic · VHS practice #1 (small)
signalment: Adult dog · breed unspecified
history: Lateral thoracic radiograph from a peer-reviewed open dataset · suited for ฝึก VHS measurement workflow
learning_objectives: ฝึก 📐 VHS 6-click workflow: long axis · short axis · vertebra ruler
สังเกต cardiac silhouette + vertebral body landmarks
เทียบค่า VHS ที่วัดได้กับ canine reference 8.5–10.5
ลอง 🪄 Auto preset ดู image contrast แล้วลอง W/L drag เอง
ลอง drag จุดที่วางผิดเพื่อปรับ — ไม่ต้องเริ่มใหม่
license: CC BY 4.0
attribution: Flores Duenas C.A., Gaxiola Camacho S.M., Montaño Gómez M.F. (2020). Radiographic Dataset for VHS determination learning process, Mendeley Data, V1, doi:10.17632/ktx4cj55pn.1 · Universidad Autónoma de Baja California

recall.final_diagnosis: Lateral thoracic — VHS measurement practice (no formal diagnosis from source)
teaching_points: Canine VHS reference range 8.5–10.5 (Buchanan & Bucheler 1995)
Long-axis from carina to apex; short-axis perpendicular at widest point
Sum vertebral lengths starting at T4 cranial edge
recall.citation: Flores Duenas C.A., Gaxiola Camacho S.M., Montaño Gómez M.F. (2020). Radiographic Dataset for VHS determination learning process, Mendeley Data, V1, doi:10.17632/ktx4cj55pn.1 · Universidad Autónoma de Baja California

title: Canine lateral thoracic · VHS practice #2 (medium)
signalment: Adult dog · breed unspecified
history: Second lateral thoracic from the same Mendeley dataset — medium-size detector area, different patient
learning_objectives: เทียบกับ case #1: VHS ของ patient ต่างกันได้ไหม? breed-related variation
ลองสลับ preset ดู bone vs soft tissue detail
ฝึกใช้ ⛶ fullscreen (F key) เพื่อมองภาพชัด ๆ
ลอง 📥 export JSON หลังวัด VHS แล้วเปิดผ่าน 🤖 Load AI ดูว่า round-trips ได้ไหม
license: CC BY 4.0
attribution: Flores Duenas C.A., Gaxiola Camacho S.M., Montaño Gómez M.F. (2020). Radiographic Dataset for VHS determination learning process, Mendeley Data, V1, doi:10.17632/ktx4cj55pn.1 · Universidad Autónoma de Baja California

recall.final_diagnosis: Lateral thoracic — VHS measurement practice (no formal diagnosis from source)
teaching_points: Inter-patient VHS variability is normal — breed conformation matters
Brachycephalic / barrel-chested breeds may run higher even when normal
recall.citation: Flores Duenas C.A., Gaxiola Camacho S.M., Montaño Gómez M.F. (2020). Radiographic Dataset for VHS determination learning process, Mendeley Data, V1, doi:10.17632/ktx4cj55pn.1 · Universidad Autónoma de Baja California

title: Canine lateral thoracic · VHS practice #3 (large-detail)
signalment: Adult dog · breed unspecified
history: Highest-resolution radiograph from the dataset — practice fine landmark identification at high zoom
learning_objectives: ใช้ 🔍 Zoom + ✋ Pan สำรวจรายละเอียดของ vertebral bodies
ลอง 📏 Length tool: วัด vertebral body length โดยตรง vs ใช้ VHS ratio
สังเกต difference ระหว่าง T4-T5 vertebrae (reference body) กับอื่น ๆ
ลอง 🔍 Info button ดู DICOM tags ของไฟล์ที่ converted มา
license: CC BY 4.0
attribution: Flores Duenas C.A., Gaxiola Camacho S.M., Montaño Gómez M.F. (2020). Radiographic Dataset for VHS determination learning process, Mendeley Data, V1, doi:10.17632/ktx4cj55pn.1 · Universidad Autónoma de Baja California

recall.final_diagnosis: Lateral thoracic — VHS measurement practice (no formal diagnosis from source)
teaching_points: Use T4 onwards as the vertebral ruler; T1–T3 obscured by shoulder
Per Buchanan: count vertebrae to nearest 0.1 (interpolate within a vertebra)
recall.citation: Flores Duenas C.A., Gaxiola Camacho S.M., Montaño Gómez M.F. (2020). Radiographic Dataset for VHS determination learning process, Mendeley Data, V1, doi:10.17632/ktx4cj55pn.1 · Universidad Autónoma de Baja California

title: Feline lateral thoracic · NORMAL (no_finding)
signalment: Cat · European Shorthair · LL projection
history: Normal lateral thoracic radiograph · ใช้เป็น reference สำหรับ feline VHS measurement (ค่าปกติ 6.7–8.1)
learning_objectives: ฝึก 📐 VHS บนแมว · auto-detect species → ใช้ feline reference 6.7–8.1 อัตโนมัติ
เปรียบเทียบ landmark กับ canine cases — แตกต่างกันยังไง?
ลอง 🪄 Auto preset ดู contrast ที่เหมาะกับ thoracic exam
ลอง 📏 Length tool: วัดความยาว diaphragm หรือ trachea diameter
license: CC BY 4.0
attribution: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

recall.final_diagnosis: Normal (no finding)
teaching_points: Feline VHS reference 6.7–8.1 (Litster & Buchanan 2000)
Cats have proportionally smaller cardiac silhouette vs dogs
recall.citation: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

title: Feline lateral thoracic · CARDIOMEGALY
signalment: Cat · breed unknown · LL projection
history: Cardiomegaly case — practice ดู VHS ที่สูงกว่าค่าปกติ (> 8.1) เปรียบเทียบกับ normal case · classic HCM (hypertrophic cardiomyopathy) consideration ในแมว
learning_objectives: วัด VHS คาดว่าจะ > 8.1 (sniffer ของ cardiomegaly)
เปรียบเทียบ cardiac silhouette กับ vetxray-feline-normal
สังเกต "Valentine heart" appearance ของ HCM ในแมว
ลอง 🦴 W/L bone/soft tissue presets ดู cardiac border ชัดสุดที่ตัวไหน
license: CC BY 4.0
attribution: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

recall.final_diagnosis: Cardiomegaly
teaching_points: HCM is the most common cardiomyopathy in cats
"Valentine heart" silhouette on DV/VD is the classic HCM marker
recall.citation: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

lesion_regions.label: enlarged cardiac silhouette
lesion_regions.hint: Compare against the feline normal case — the enlarged heart fills more intercostal spaces and pushes the trachea dorsally. "Valentine heart" on DV is the HCM tell.

title: Feline lateral thoracic · PLEURAL EFFUSION
signalment: Cat · European Shorthair · LL projection
history: Pleural effusion case · เห็น fluid line ใน pleural space · cardiac silhouette อาจดู obscured · VHS วัดยากกว่า normal
learning_objectives: สังเกต pleural fluid pattern (interlobar fissures · diaphragm border)
ลอง VHS — เมื่อมี effusion, cardiac silhouette ถูก partially obscured · VHS reliability ต่ำลง
ฝึกใช้ 🔍 Zoom ดู costophrenic angle ที่ blunted
ลอง preset Bone vs Soft ดู fluid contrast
license: CC BY 4.0
attribution: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

findings: Pleural fluid visible in interlobar fissures
recall.final_diagnosis: Pleural effusion
teaching_points: VHS reliability drops when cardiac border is obscured by fluid
Always look for interlobar fissure lines as the giveaway sign
recall.citation: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

title: Feline lateral thoracic · INTERSTITIAL PATTERN
signalment: แมว · LL projection · European Shorthair
history: Interstitial pattern · increased lung opacity without alveolar consolidation
learning_objectives: แยก unstructured (diffuse haze) vs structured (nodular/reticular)
DDx: pulmonary fibrosis, neoplasia, early pulmonary edema, infection
เปรียบเทียบกับ alveolar pattern case
license: CC BY 4.0
attribution: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

findings: Increased lung opacity without alveolar consolidation
recall.final_diagnosis: Interstitial pattern
teaching_points: Structured (nodular/reticular) vs unstructured (diffuse haze) sub-categorization narrows DDx
Interstitial is the broadest DDx among lung patterns — always pair with clinical history
recall.citation: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

title: Feline lateral thoracic · ALVEOLAR PATTERN
signalment: แมว · LL projection · European Shorthair
history: Alveolar pattern in cat · DDx pneumonia, edema
learning_objectives: สังเกต air bronchograms ในแมว
compare กับ canine alveolar pattern case
feline alveolar DDx: viral/bacterial pneumonia, CHF
license: CC BY 4.0
attribution: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

recall.final_diagnosis: Alveolar pattern
teaching_points: Air bronchograms = alveolar consolidation by definition
In cats, CHF often presents with patchy alveolar pattern rather than the classic perihilar canine distribution
recall.citation: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

title: Feline lateral thoracic · BRONCHIAL PATTERN
signalment: แมว · LL projection · European Shorthair
history: Bronchial pattern · thickened bronchial walls · classic feline asthma
learning_objectives: สังเกต "donut" + "tram-line" signs ของ thickened bronchi
ระบุ distribution: focal · diffuse
ใน cats: bronchial pattern + hyperinflation = consider feline asthma
license: CC BY 4.0
attribution: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

findings: "Donut" sign — thickened bronchial walls on end
"Tram-line" sign — thickened bronchial walls in long axis
recall.final_diagnosis: Bronchial pattern
teaching_points: Bronchial pattern + hyperinflation in a cat = feline asthma until proven otherwise
Donut (end-on) and tram-line (long-axis) are two views of the same thickened wall
recall.citation: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

title: Feline lateral thoracic · MASS
signalment: แมว · LL projection · Persian
history: Soft tissue mass · feline cardiothoracic mass DDx
learning_objectives: ระบุ mass location, size, borders
วัดขนาดด้วย 📏 Length tool
DDx: lymphoma, thymoma, neoplasia ของ lung
license: CC BY 4.0
attribution: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

recall.final_diagnosis: Mass
teaching_points: Mediastinal lymphoma is the most common cranial mediastinal mass in young cats
Always measure mass dimensions for staging and treatment follow-up
recall.citation: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

lesion_regions.label: cranial mediastinal mass
lesion_regions.hint: Mediastinal masses in cats classically occupy the cranio-ventral mediastinum — thymoma (older cats) and lymphoma (young cats) are the top DDx.

title: Feline lateral thoracic · PNEUMOTHORAX
signalment: แมว · LL projection
history: Pneumothorax · free air in pleural space · EMERGENCY
learning_objectives: สังเกต lung edge displaced จาก thoracic wall · radiolucent space
ระบุ tension vs simple pneumothorax (mediastinal shift?)
Emergency consideration — usually requires intervention
license: CC BY 4.0
attribution: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

findings: Lung edge displaced from thoracic wall
Radiolucent (gas) space between lung and parietal pleura
recall.final_diagnosis: Pneumothorax
teaching_points: Tension pneumothorax = mediastinal shift away from affected side — surgical emergency
Treat the patient, not the radiograph — clinical signs override imaging when patient is decompensating
recall.citation: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

lesion_regions.label: dorsal pleural air gap
lesion_regions.hint: On a lateral, look for a radiolucent (black) band between the lung edge and the thoracic spine, and an elevated cardiac silhouette off the sternum.

title: Canine lateral thoracic · CARDIOMEGALY
signalment: หมา · LL projection · breed unspecified

learning_objectives: วัด VHS — คาดเกิน 10.5 (canine cardiomegaly threshold)
เปรียบเทียบ cardiac silhouette กับ vetxray-canine-normal
สังเกต breed-specific cardiac shape variations
license: CC BY 4.0
attribution: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

recall.final_diagnosis: Cardiomegaly
teaching_points: MMVD is the #1 acquired cardiac disease in dogs, especially small breeds
DCM more common in large breeds (Doberman, Great Dane, Boxer)
recall.citation: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

lesion_regions.label: enlarged cardiac silhouette
lesion_regions.hint: VHS workflow tells you HOW MUCH the heart is enlarged. Spot-the-finding tells you WHERE the cardiac silhouette is on the image. Use both.

title: Canine lateral thoracic · PLEURAL EFFUSION

history: Pleural effusion · fluid line · cardiac silhouette obscured
learning_objectives: สังเกต pleural fluid patterns: interlobar fissures · blunted costophrenic angle
VHS reliability ลดลงเมื่อ cardiac border obscured
ลอง Bone vs Soft preset ดู fluid contrast

attribution: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

recall.final_diagnosis: Pleural effusion
teaching_points: DDx for effusion in dogs differs from cats — neoplasia, CHF, pyothorax, chylothorax
Always interpret VHS with caution when the cardiac border is silhouetted by fluid

title: Canine lateral thoracic · MASS
signalment: หมา · LL projection
history: Soft tissue mass · DDx primary vs metastatic
learning_objectives: ระบุ mass location · borders · sharpness
วัดขนาด mass ด้วย 📏 Length tool (mm calibrated)
สังเกต relationship กับ surrounding structures
license: CC BY 4.0
attribution: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

recall.final_diagnosis: Mass
teaching_points: Solitary well-defined mass favors primary tumor; multiple small nodules favor metastatic
Follow up with CT for staging — radiographs miss ~30% of metastatic nodules
recall.citation: VetXRay — 9,882 manually annotated canine and feline thoracic radiographs · Zenodo · DOI:10.5281/zenodo.19051776 · CC BY 4.0

lesion_regions.label: caudo-dorsal lung field (search zone)
lesion_regions.hint: Without a lobe-specific label, the caudo-dorsal lung field is the canonical search region for canine primary pulmonary mass. Compare against the canine-normal lung pattern.

title: Canine lateral thoracic · ALVEOLAR PATTERN

title: Canine extended-leg pelvis VD · Norberg practice (CUVET)
signalment: Dog · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Extended-leg ventrodorsal pelvis from the CUVET teaching archive — the standard projection for coxofemoral evaluation and Norberg-angle practice. Image anonymized via the CUVET 4-pass scrubber pipeline; institutional R anatomy marker preserved.
learning_objectives: ฝึก 📐 Norberg angle 4-click workflow บนภาพจริงจาก CUVET PACS
ระบุ landmark: femoral head + acetabular rim + obturator foramen
ลอง 🪄 Auto preset ดู bone contrast แล้ว drag W/L เอง
เปรียบเทียบ extended-leg position vs alternative views (ไม่มีในชุดนี้)
อ่าน R anatomy marker เพื่อยืนยัน left vs right hip
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

findings: Symmetric femoral-head placement in acetabula on visual inspection
R anatomy marker confirms right-side orientation
recall.final_diagnosis: Visually normal extended-leg pelvis (no formal radiologist read available for this anonymized teaching case — practice the measurement workflow, not the diagnosis)
teaching_points: Norberg angle measures coxofemoral subluxation severity — vertex at the femoral head, lines to centre + craniolateral acetabular rim
Reference cut-offs: ≥105° normal · 95–105° borderline · <95° dysplastic (Smith et al 1990 PennHIP-adjacent)
Always measure BOTH hips and compare — asymmetry is a clinical flag even when individual angles look OK
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Canine lateral thorax · VHS practice (CUVET)
signalment: Dog · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Lateral thoracic radiograph from the CUVET teaching archive. Cardiac silhouette, tracheal column, and diaphragmatic line in profile — VHS-compatible view. Image anonymized via the CUVET 4-pass scrubber pipeline.
learning_objectives: ฝึก 📐 VHS 6-click workflow บนภาพจริงจาก CUVET PACS
สังเกต cardiac silhouette + vertebral body landmarks เริ่มที่ T4
เทียบ VHS ที่วัดได้กับ canine reference 8.5–10.5 (Buchanan & Bücheler 1995)
ลอง 🪄 Auto preset ดู cardiac contrast
ลอง drag จุดที่วางผิดเพื่อปรับ — ไม่ต้องเริ่มใหม่
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

findings: Cardiac silhouette visible in lateral profile
Tracheal column visible dorsal to heart
Vertebral bodies counted from T4 onwards
recall.final_diagnosis: Lateral thoracic projection — VHS measurement practice (no formal radiologist read available for this anonymized case)
teaching_points: Canine VHS reference 8.5–10.5 (Buchanan & Bücheler 1995)
Long-axis from carina to apex; short-axis perpendicular at widest point
Sum vertebral lengths starting at T4 cranial edge
Breed conformation modifies the upper bound — Labradors and barrel-chested breeds can sit 10.5–11 while normal
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Canine pelvis + bilateral stifle VD (CUVET)
signalment: Dog · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Ventrodorsal pelvis extended caudally to capture both stifle joints — femoral condyles, tibial plateaus, and fabellae visible on both sides. Useful for stifle-symmetry screening and as a one-image hindlimb reference. Image anonymized via the CUVET 4-pass scrubber pipeline.
learning_objectives: วัด Norberg angle บนทั้งสองข้างแล้วเปรียบเทียบ symmetry
ระบุ landmarks ของ stifle: femoral condyles · tibial plateau · fabellae
ลอง 📏 Length tool: วัดระยะ joint space เทียบสองข้าง
ฝึกอ่าน asymmetry signals ที่อาจชี้ pathology แม้ค่าเดี่ยวจะปกติ
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

findings: Pelvic landmarks symmetric on visual inspection
recall.final_diagnosis: Combined pelvis + bilateral stifle VD — measurement practice (no formal radiologist read for this anonymized case)
teaching_points: Norberg reference cut-offs: ≥105° normal · 95–105° borderline · <95° dysplastic
Stifle symmetry between left and right is itself a screening signal — compare joint spaces and fabella positions
Extended-leg position is required for accurate Norberg; this is a single-image hindlimb reference, not a substitute for dedicated stifle laterals
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Canine lateral abdomen (CUVET)
signalment: Dog · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Lateral abdominal radiograph from the CUVET teaching archive. Vertebral column dorsally, intestinal pattern and serosal silhouettes ventrally, pelvic inlet caudally. Image anonymized via the CUVET 4-pass scrubber pipeline.
learning_objectives: ฝึกอ่าน serosal detail + bowel gas pattern บนภาพจริง
ระบุ vertebral column · diaphragm · pelvic inlet เป็น landmark boundaries
ลอง 📏 Length tool: วัด vertebral body หรือ kidney shadow ถ้าเห็น
เทียบกับ VD view ที่ /cases/cuvet-canine-pelvis-stifle-vd-001
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

findings: Vertebral column visible across dorsal aspect
Diaphragm + caudal liver silhouette visible cranially
Mixed bowel gas pattern across mid-abdomen
Pelvic inlet visible at caudal margin
recall.final_diagnosis: Lateral abdomen — anatomical reference (no formal radiologist read for this anonymized case)
teaching_points: Lateral + VD pair is the standard 2-view abdomen — one view alone misses lateralized findings
Serosal detail depends on intra-abdominal fat; thin patients show less contrast naturally
Always trace organ outlines systematically: liver → stomach → small bowel → colon → bladder → kidneys
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Canine VD abdomen — paired view (CUVET)
signalment: Dog · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Ventrodorsal abdomen from the CUVET teaching archive — pair view for cuvet-canine-abdomen-lat-001. Lumbar vertebrae down midline, paired renal silhouettes, intestinal gas pattern, sacroiliac + femoral heads at pelvic inlet, L anatomy marker top-right.
learning_objectives: ฝึก 2-view abdomen evaluation: เปรียบเทียบกับ /cases/cuvet-canine-abdomen-lat-001
ระบุ renal silhouettes ที่อยู่ข้างกระดูกสันหลัง L1–L3
อ่าน L anatomy marker เพื่อยืนยัน orientation
ลอง 📏 Length tool: วัด renal long-axis (canine kidney ~2.5–3.5 × L2 length)
สำรวจ bowel pattern เปรียบเทียบ left vs right
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

findings: Sacroiliac joints + femoral heads at caudal margin
recall.final_diagnosis: VD abdomen — paired-view anatomy reference (no formal radiologist read for this anonymized case)
teaching_points: Renal long-axis : L2 vertebral length ratio is the canonical kidney-size screen (canine 2.5–3.5×)
VD view reveals lateralized findings that lateral hides (e.g. unilateral renomegaly, asymmetric bowel)
Always read VD + lateral together — single view = incomplete read
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Canine elbow lateral (CUVET)
signalment: Dog · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Lateral elbow (humero-radio-ulnar joint) from the CUVET teaching archive. Joint flexed at ~110°, with humeral condyle proximally, anconeal process projecting behind the joint, and radius + ulna distally. Body-wall silhouette visible caudo-distally. Image anonymized via the CUVET 4-pass scrubber pipeline.
learning_objectives: ระบุ landmarks: humeral condyle · anconeal process · radial head · olecranon
ลอง 📏 Length tool: วัด joint space ที่ humero-radial articulation
ลอง 📐 Angle tool: วัดมุม flexion ของ elbow
สังเกต anatomy marker R/L เพื่อยืนยัน orientation
เปรียบเทียบกับ /cases/cuvet-canine-forelimb-lat-001 (distal antebrachium pair)

findings: Humeral condyle visible as the proximal articular surface
Anconeal process (ulna) projecting behind the joint space
Radius head + ulna distal to the joint
recall.final_diagnosis: Lateral elbow — anatomy reference (no formal radiologist read for this anonymized case)
teaching_points: Anconeal process should be smoothly continuous with the olecranon — fragmentation = developmental elbow disease (UAP)
Joint space should be uniform; asymmetric narrowing is an OA / DJD signal
Routine elbow exam needs lateral + craniocaudal views — single lateral can hide medial coronoid disease (FCP)
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Canine forelimb lateral · antebrachium + carpus (CUVET)
signalment: Dog · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Lateral antebrachium (radius + ulna) and carpus from the CUVET teaching archive. Full forearm shaft visible with carpal joints at the distal end. R anatomy marker top-left.
learning_objectives: ระบุ radius vs ulna (radius = cranial slimmer · ulna = caudal with olecranon proximal end)
อ่าน carpal-row anatomy: proximal + distal rows · 7 carpal bones
ลอง 📏 Length tool: วัด radius + ulna shaft lengths (compare ratio)
เปรียบเทียบกับ /cases/cuvet-canine-elbow-lat-001 (proximal joint pair)
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

findings: Carpal bones in proximal + distal rows
recall.final_diagnosis: Lateral antebrachium + carpus — anatomy reference (no formal radiologist read for this anonymized case)
teaching_points: Radius bears most of the axial load; ulna's olecranon transmits triceps pull
Carpal hyperextension injuries are common in agility / working dogs — palpate joint laxity even when radiograph looks normal
Growth-plate (physis) timing: distal radial physis closes ~9-12mo in canines · earlier closure = growth disturbance
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Feline skull DV (CUVET) · first feline DICOM case
signalment: Cat · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Dorsoventral skull from the CUVET teaching archive — first feline DICOM case in the catalog. Symmetric tympanic bullae, dental arcades, zygomatic arches. Lead L marker (CUVET institutional) visible top-left.
learning_objectives: ระบุ feline skull landmarks: tympanic bullae · zygomatic arches · dental arcades
เปรียบเทียบ symmetry ซ้าย vs ขวา — asymmetry คือ early screening signal
ลอง 📏 Length tool: วัด zygomatic arch span (breed shape reference)
อ่าน lead L marker เพื่อยืนยัน orientation (CUVET ใช้ L = animal's left)
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

findings: Tympanic bullae paired and symmetric (small ovoid lucencies lateral)
recall.final_diagnosis: Feline DV skull — anatomy reference (no formal radiologist read for this anonymized case)
teaching_points: Asymmetric tympanic bullae = early sign of otitis media or nasopharyngeal disease
DV is the standard for bullae comparison; open-mouth view supplements for tympanic detail
Brachycephalic cats (Persian, Exotic Shorthair) show shortened skulls — breed conformation modifies what 'normal' looks like
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Feline skull lateral (CUVET)
signalment: Cat · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Lateral skull from the CUVET teaching archive — rounded cranial vault and short rostrum typical of feline conformation. Pair with /cases/cuvet-feline-skull-dv-001 for two-view skull evaluation. (Cephalic conformation not asserted: breed unknown, single anonymized view.)
learning_objectives: ระบุ feline skull landmarks: rounded cranial vault · short rostrum · tympanic bullae · dental arcade
เปรียบเทียบกับ /cases/cuvet-canine-skull-lat-001 (longer-muzzled canine)
ลอง 📏 Length tool: วัด skull length vs cranial width (cephalic index)
อ่าน tympanic bullae ในมุม lateral (superimposed left + right)
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

findings: Tympanic bullae superimposed at skull base
recall.final_diagnosis: Feline lateral skull — anatomy reference (no formal radiologist read for this anonymized case; cephalic conformation not classified from one view)
teaching_points: Cephalic conformation (dolicho / meso / brachycephalic) is breed-dependent — classify by the cephalic index below, not by eye on a single view
Lateral skull alone superimposes tympanic bullae — use DV or oblique views to separate L/R bullae
Cephalic index (length / width) is the formal classification: brachy < 80, meso 80-90, dolicho > 90
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Canine skull lateral (CUVET)
signalment: Dog · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Lateral skull from the CUVET teaching archive — elongated muzzle with complete adult dental arcades (longer-faced conformation). Useful contrast against the shorter-faced feline lateral skull for skull-shape comparison. (Specific cephalic class not asserted: breed unknown, single anonymized view.)
learning_objectives: ระบุ canine skull landmarks: elongated muzzle · full adult dental arcade · tympanic bullae
เปรียบเทียบกับ /cases/cuvet-feline-skull-lat-001 (shorter-faced feline)
อ่าน dental arcades: 42 adult teeth ทั้งหมด (3-1-4-2 / 3-1-4-3)
ลอง 📏 Length tool: วัด muzzle vs cranium length (cephalic index)
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

recall.final_diagnosis: Canine lateral skull — anatomy reference (no formal radiologist read for this anonymized case; specific cephalic class not asserted from one view)
teaching_points: Adult canine dentition = 42 teeth (3-1-4-2 maxilla / 3-1-4-3 mandible · I-C-P-M)
Dolichocephalic (Greyhound · Borzoi) breeds have longer muzzles — distinct from mesocephalic (Labrador · Mixed) by cephalic index
Tympanic bullae thinner in dogs than cats — paired view (DV / open-mouth) needed for asymmetry assessment
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Canine thoraco-lumbar spine VD (CUVET)
signalment: Dog · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Thoraco-lumbar spine VD from the CUVET teaching archive. Full thoracic + cranial lumbar vertebrae with ribs symmetric on both sides. Useful for vertebrae counting, alignment screening, and intervertebral disc space uniformity.
learning_objectives: นับ vertebrae: 13 thoracic (T1–T13) + อย่างน้อย L1–L5 ใน view นี้
ระบุ ribs ที่ attach แต่ละ thoracic vertebra (symmetric paired)
ลอง 📏 Length tool: วัด disc space uniformity (compare L1-L2 vs L2-L3 etc.)
อ่าน spinous processes กลางลำตัว: ค้นหา deviation จาก midline
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

findings: Thoracic vertebrae visible with paired ribs attached
Lumbar vertebrae (cranial portion visible — frame cuts off mid-lumbar)
Ribs symmetric on left and right
Spinous processes as midline column of densities
recall.final_diagnosis: Thoraco-lumbar spine VD — anatomy reference (no formal radiologist read for this anonymized case)
teaching_points: Canine has 13 thoracic + 7 lumbar vertebrae · always count ribs to confirm anatomy
Disc space narrowing on VD often visible before lateral view — bridge that read across both projections
VD is screening; lateral is confirmatory for vertebral body shape and disc-space height
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Canine thorax VD · paired view (CUVET)
signalment: Dog · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Ventrodorsal thorax from the CUVET teaching archive — pair view for /cases/cuvet-canine-thorax-lat-001. Symmetric ribs left + right of midline, cardiac silhouette centred, spine running down midline.
learning_objectives: ฝึก 2-view thorax evaluation: เปรียบเทียบกับ /cases/cuvet-canine-thorax-lat-001
อ่าน cardiac silhouette symmetry (VD = paired heart-shape rules)
ระบุ ribs ทั้ง left + right ที่ symmetric
ลอง 📏 Length tool: วัด cardiac silhouette width vs thoracic width (CTR · cardiothoracic ratio)
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

recall.final_diagnosis: Canine thorax VD — paired-view anatomy reference (no formal radiologist read for this anonymized case)
teaching_points: VHS reference standard is the LATERAL view (Buchanan 1995) — VD is supplementary
Cardiothoracic ratio (CTR) on VD is less species-specific than VHS but useful for trend monitoring
Lateralized findings (one lung field, asymmetric cardiac silhouette) require the VD view to detect — never read one view alone
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Feline lateral thorax · VHS practice (CUVET)
signalment: Cat · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Lateral thoracic radiograph from the CUVET teaching archive. Slim body wall, more horizontal trachea, and elongated cardiac silhouette characteristic of feline conformation. Useful for feline VHS practice (reference 6.7–8.1, Litster & Buchanan 2000) — distinct workflow from canine due to smaller VHS range. Image anonymized via the CUVET 4-pass scrubber pipeline.
learning_objectives: ฝึก 📐 VHS workflow บน feline anatomy (auto-detect species → feline reference 6.7–8.1)
เปรียบเทียบ feline vs canine cardiac silhouette shape (elongated vs rounded)
สังเกต tracheal column ที่ horizontal มากกว่า canine
ระบุ landmarks: cardiac silhouette · trachea · diaphragm · vertebrae T4 onwards
เปรียบเทียบกับ /cases/cuvet-canine-thorax-lat-001 (canine equivalent)
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

findings: Cardiac silhouette in feline lateral profile (elongated shape)
Tracheal column visible (more horizontal than canine)
Vertebral bodies visible across dorsal aspect (T4 onwards)
recall.final_diagnosis: Feline lateral thorax — VHS measurement practice (no formal radiologist read available for this anonymized case)
teaching_points: Feline VHS reference 6.7–8.1 (Litster & Buchanan 2000) — different from canine range
"Valentine heart" appearance on DV is the HCM tell — but lateral is the VHS workflow standard
Feline cardiac silhouette has different orientation than canine — measure on the actual long-axis, not assumed orientation
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Canine cervical spine lateral (CUVET)
signalment: Dog · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Cervicothoracic lateral from the CUVET teaching archive (source study labeled 'Chest'). The cervical vertebral column C1–C7 is clearly visible curving from the skull base through the cervicothoracic junction into the cranial thorax — suited for cervical vertebra identification and intervertebral disc-space practice. Image anonymized via the CUVET 4-pass scrubber pipeline.
learning_objectives: นับ + ระบุ cervical vertebrae C1 (atlas) → C7 ตาม ventral curve
ระบุ atlantoaxial region (C1–C2) ที่อยู่ใกล้ skull base
ลอง 📏 Length tool: วัด intervertebral disc spaces เทียบกันแต่ละช่อง
หา cervicothoracic junction (C7–T1) ที่ neck เปลี่ยนเป็น thorax
เปรียบเทียบกับ /cases/cuvet-canine-spine-tl-vd-001 (thoracolumbar spine view)
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

findings: Cervical vertebral bodies C1–C7 in a ventral curve
Atlas (C1) + axis (C2) near the skull base
Trachea as a lucent column ventral to the spine
Cranial thoracic vertebrae + ribs entering the field
recall.final_diagnosis: Canine cervicothoracic lateral — cervical spine anatomy + disc-space practice (no formal radiologist read for this anonymized case)
teaching_points: Cervical disc spaces are normally fairly uniform; a single narrowed space can be a Hansen type-II / IVDD signal — but confirm on a well-positioned, non-rotated lateral first
C1–C2 (atlantoaxial) is the site of AA instability, especially in toy breeds — flexed views assess it, but never force flexion in a suspected case
A true lateral superimposes the wings of the atlas + paired transverse processes; rotation mimics disease, so check positioning before reading any pathology
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Canine pelvis · lateral + VD (CUVET)
signalment: Dog · breed unknown · CUVET teaching set
history: Two-view pelvis study from the CUVET teaching archive (study labeled 'Hip-joint'): a lateral pelvic-limb projection (hip + femur + stifle) paired with an extended-leg VD pelvis (both coxofemoral joints + femora). Suited for paired-view hip evaluation and practising the Norberg-angle workflow on the VD. Images anonymized via the CUVET 4-pass scrubber pipeline.
learning_objectives: ฝึก paired-view hip eval: lateral + VD ของ pelvis เดียวกัน
ระบุ landmarks: femoral head · acetabulum · greater trochanter · stifle (ใน lateral view)
ลอง 📐 Norberg workflow บน VD (วาง vertices ที่ femoral heads + dorsal acetabular rims) — ฝึก workflow เท่านั้น, ค่าจริงรอ radiologist read
เทียบ symmetry ของ coxofemoral joints ซ้าย-ขวา บน VD
เปรียบเทียบกับ /cases/cuvet-canine-pelvis-vd-001 (Norberg practice case)
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

findings: Lateral: hip joint, femur, and stifle in one pelvic-limb projection
VD: both coxofemoral joints (femoral head + acetabulum), symmetric
VD: both femora with long axis visible
Lumbosacral junction cranial to the pelvis
recall.final_diagnosis: Canine 2-view pelvis (lateral + VD) — paired-view anatomy + Norberg workflow practice (no formal radiologist read for this anonymized case)
teaching_points: Norberg angle is measured on the extended-leg VD — never the lateral; the lateral gives sacroiliac / luxation context
A symmetric, well-positioned VD is mandatory before reading hip laxity — pelvic tilt mimics subluxation
Coxofemoral OA signs (osteophytes, remodelled femoral head, shallow acetabulum) need both views to characterise
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

title: Canine stifle · post-operative, tibial implant (CUVET)
signalment: Dog · ~16 kg · CUVET teaching set
history: Post-operative canine stifle (study labeled 'KNEE JNT … CANINE'). The craniocaudal view shows a proximal tibial bone plate with cortical screws; the lateral view shows the femorotibial joint with patella and fabella and no implant in that projection. First hindlimb-stifle and first orthopedic-implant case in the catalog. Images anonymized via the CUVET 4-pass scrubber pipeline.
learning_objectives: ระบุ orthopedic implant: proximal tibial plate + cortical screws บน CrCd view
ระบุ stifle landmarks บน lateral: patella · fabella · femoral condyles · tibial plateau · tibial tuberosity
เข้าใจว่าทำไม implant ต้องดู ≥2 views (CrCd เห็น plate ชัด · lateral อาจไม่เห็น)
ลอง 📏 Length / 📐 Angle tools รอบ joint + implant
เทียบ projection: CrCd vs lateral ของ stifle เดียวกัน
license: Educational use, CUVET-internal · anonymized with Aj. approval
attribution: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission

findings: CrCd: proximal tibial bone plate fixed with cortical screws
CrCd: femorotibial joint + crus + tarsus in the same projection
Lateral: femoral condyles, femorotibial joint space, tibial plateau
Lateral: implant not profiled in this projection
recall.final_diagnosis: Post-operative canine stifle with a proximal tibial implant — orthopedic teaching case (no formal radiologist read for this anonymized case; identify the hardware + landmarks, do not assume the indication)
teaching_points: A proximal tibial plate has several common indications (e.g. TPLO for cranial-cruciate disease, or tibial fracture repair) — the exact procedure is NOT inferable from a single anonymized study without the record
Implant + fracture/osteotomy assessment ALWAYS needs orthogonal views — a plate obvious on CrCd can sit out of profile on the lateral
Patella + fabellae are the lateral-stifle anchors; loss of the fabellae or a displaced patella changes the whole interpretation
recall.citation: Courtesy of Small Animal Hospital of Chulalongkorn University · DI Unit · anonymized for veterinary education with permission
